learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Loops, Prefix Sum, Time Complexity, Space Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine a balanced weighing scale where numbers on the left side represent weights on one pan and numbers on the right side represent weights on the other. The pivot index is the position where both sides have exactly the same total weight.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine a balanced weighing scale where numbers on the left side represent weights on one pan and numbers on the right side represent weights on the other. The pivot index is the position where both sides have exactly the same total weight.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use this Running Prefix Sum pattern whenever a problem requires comparing cumulative sums on both sides of every index.
learning.signals: Whenever a problem asks you to compare the cumulative sum on the left and right side of every index, think of Running Prefix Sum.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given an integer array nums, return the leftmost pivot index. The pivot index is the index where the sum of all numbers strictly to the left is equal to the sum of all numbers strictly to the right. If no such index exists, return -1.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: For every index, separately compute the sum of elements on the left and right sides.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: Instead of calculating left and right sums repeatedly for every index, first compute the total sum. As you move through the array, maintain a running left sum. The right sum can then be calculated instantly as totalSum - leftSum - currentElement.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Right sum can always be computed using Total Sum - Left Sum - Current Element.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever a problem asks you to compare the cumulative sum on the left and right side of every index, think of Running Prefix Sum.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Each element is processed only once after computing the total sum. The running left sum eliminates the need to repeatedly calculate left and right sums.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever a problem asks you to compare the cumulative sum on the left and right side of every index, think of Running Prefix Sum.
commonWrongPaths: Including the current element in the left or right sum.
Updating left sum before checking equality.
Forgetting to subtract the current element from the total.
Returning the last pivot instead of the first.

hints: Right sum can be computed without another loop.
commonMisconceptions: Including the current element in the left or right sum.
Updating left sum before checking equality.
Forgetting to subtract the current element from the total.
Returning the last pivot instead of the first.

pattern.name: Running Prefix Sum
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever a problem asks you to compare the cumulative sum on the left and right side of every index, think of Running Prefix Sum.

approaches.title: Calculate Left and Right Sum for Every Index
approaches.idea: For every index, separately compute the sum of elements on the left and right sides.
algorithm: If both are equal, return the index.
approaches.dryRun:
nums=[1,7,3,6,5,6]
Index0 → 0 vs 27
Index1 → 1 vs 20
Index3 → 11 vs 11 ✓

approaches.title: Prefix Sum Array
approaches.idea: Build a prefix sum array so left and right sums can be computed in constant time.
algorithm: For each index compute left sum.
Compute right sum using total sum.

approaches.title: Running Prefix Sum
approaches.idea: First compute the total sum of the array. Traverse the array while maintaining a running left sum. At every index, calculate the right sum as totalSum - leftSum - currentElement. If both sums are equal, return the current index.
approaches.intuition: Each element is processed only once after computing the total sum. The running left sum eliminates the need to repeatedly calculate left and right sums.
approaches.dryRun:
nums=[1,7,3,6,5,6]
Total=28
Index0: Left=0 Right=27
Index1: Left=1 Right=20
Index2: Left=8 Right=17
Index3: Left=11 Right=11 ✓ Return 3

visualization.description: Maintain the left sum while computing the right sum using the total sum.

interviewFollowUps: How would you find all pivot indices?
Can this be solved using a Prefix Sum array?
What if the array changes dynamically?
How is this related to Running Sum problems?
interviewTips: Mention why recomputing sums is inefficient.
Explain the Total - Left - Current formula.
Update left sum only after checking.
State O(n) time and O(1) space.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Right sum can always be computed using Total Sum - Left Sum - Current Element.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Total Sum → Running Left Sum → Compare Left & Right.

importantPoints: Current element belongs to neither side.
commonMistakes: Including the current element in the left or right sum.
Updating left sum before checking equality.
Forgetting to subtract the current element from the total.
Returning the last pivot instead of the first.

relatedProblems: Find the Middle Index in Array
